person.name: Cho Seokgyu
person.title: 15-Year Backend Engineer
person.summary: A 15-year veteran backend engineer who has experienced the full cycle from service design to large-scale traffic handling and operations. Led performance optimization and architecture design across diverse domains — from real-time game servers and B2B SaaS platforms to AI-based services.
highlights: PHP → Go migration: 100x throughput improvement (30 → 3,000 req/s), 50% infrastructure cost reduction
Scale-out handling 40,000 req/s massive traffic (Dragon Nest Wonderland)
Query optimization: partner revenue management response time 5 min → instant
As CTO, led team building, CI/CD setup, and architecture design for 6 years 9 months
Currently developing polyglot (Node.js/Python/Kotlin) services in K8s microservices environment

company-muple.name: Muple Inc.
company-muple.description: Fullstack development of Murfy, an AI-based LaTeX Web IDE. Designing and operating microservices in Kubernetes with a polyglot backend (Node.js/Python/Kotlin) and Vue-based frontend.
achievements: Microservice design and operation in Kubernetes environment
Polyglot microservice architecture with Node.js, Python, Kotlin
Yjs-based real-time collaborative editing integration
PDF viewer implementation
CloudWatch CPU-based Slack alerts and build failure notifications
Solo migration of 1-year Python codebase to TypeScript in 1 week using AI pipeline

company-bebecook.name: Bebecook
company-bebecook.description: Migrated Azure MSSQL-based system to AWS MySQL and established a CI/CD pipeline.
achievements: Migrated Azure MSSQL + stored procedure-based system to AWS MySQL
Resolved cross-apply/pivot gaps, procedure return type differences, and collation mismatches
Moved stored procedure logic to application layer, improving maintainability and visibility
Established CI/CD pipeline
lessons: Without defining acceptance criteria and exit conditions at the contract stage, you can solve every technical problem and still massively overrun — define done before you start

company-revivallabs.name: Revival Labs
company-revivallabs.description: Designed, developed, and operated 'KongKong', an event management B2B platform. Achieved 100x throughput improvement and 50% infrastructure cost reduction by migrating from PHP to Go. Built and shipped 30+ feature modules over 7 years.
achievements: PHP → Go migration: 100x performance improvement (30 → 3,000 req/s, single-core 512MB RAM)
50% monthly infrastructure cost reduction
Go channel-based event-driven architecture to minimize service coupling
Designed and shipped 30+ feature modules for event operators and attendees
Docker scale-out, Redis caching, WebSocket real-time dashboard
CI/CD pipeline enabling bi-weekly continuous deployment
Grew 2 non-CS team members to independent full-stack developers over 7 years
lessons: Skipping tests and observability early compounds over time — bug fixes became increasingly costly and releases slowed as the codebase grew
Bus factor 1 is fundamentally a knowledge transfer failure, not just a stamina problem — when structure is concentrated in one person, their departure breaks the system
B2B event platforms face structural scaling limits: each customer's requirements are subtly different, making true platformization hard without LLM-level adaptability

company-theswing.name: The Swing
company-theswing.description: Unified payment system by abstracting multiple payment gateway logics behind a common interface.
achievements: Abstracted multiple PG payment logics into a common interface
Technical lectures for junior developers (once a week, 4 weeks)

company-kolon.name: Kolon Mall (PurpleWorks)
company-kolon.description: Built an asynchronous data migration pipeline with AWS SQS + Lambda, implemented a React-based back-office admin console, and migrated SAP delivery functionality to a Spring application.
achievements: Built async large-scale review data migration pipeline with AWS SQS + Lambda
Designed and implemented React-based back-office admin console
Migrated SAP delivery functionality to Spring-based application
Micronaut framework R&D

company-octave.name: Octave Games
company-octave.description: Developed client and server for Facebook social casino game 'Suites in Vegas'. Led the team as a founding member.
achievements: Flash/ActionScript game client development
Java/Spring game server and admin console design/development
Led team as founding member
Built Jenkins-based CI/CD pipeline
Introduced TDD

company-carrot.name: Carrot Games
company-carrot.description: Developed server for mobile MMORPG 'Rebirth M'. Built real-time game server for global launch.
achievements: Multi-threaded real-time game server design and implementation
TCP/IP socket communication and concurrent connection management

company-cube.name: Cube Games
company-cube.description: Developed server and Unity3D client network library for mobile board game 'Dragon Nest Wonderland'. Handled massive traffic of 40,000 req/s through scale-out.
achievements: Handled 40,000 req/s massive traffic via scale-out
Multi-threaded TCP/IP game server and Unity3D client network library design
Solved iOS Mono generic limitations with a Python code generator

company-kidari.name: Kidari Studio
company-kidari.description: Developed server for Facebook social game 'Secret Garden'. Implemented game server and admin console for global launch.
achievements: Game server and admin console development
Global launch support

company-groupon.name: Groupon Korea
company-groupon.description: Shopping mall backend development. Identified the root cause of partner revenue management data inconsistency and switched to a Spring Batch-based confirmed data storage structure, reducing response time from 5+ minutes to instant.
achievements: Identified root cause: real-time aggregation mixing finalized and live data caused both performance and consistency issues
Switched to Spring Batch-based confirmed data storage, eliminating query-time dependency
Response time 5 min+ → instant; data consistency restored

company-airforce.name: ROKAF Central Computing Center
company-airforce.description: Developed a win-loss management system for Air Force game team 'ACE'.
achievements: Developed ACE team win-loss management system
Java/JSP-based web system implementation

project-latex-ide.name: Murfy (AI LaTeX Web IDE)
project-latex-ide.description: Backend for Murfy, an AI-based LaTeX web editor. Microservice architecture in Kubernetes with polyglot services across multiple languages.
achievements: K8s microservice design and operation
Node.js/Python/Kotlin polyglot service development
Yjs-based real-time collaborative editing integration
PDF viewer implementation
CloudWatch CPU-based Slack alert setup
Container orchestration and deployment management
lessons: Forcing stateful services (Yjs documents) into a stateless K8s architecture made scale-out impossible — sandbox-per-project from the start would have eliminated the problem entirely
Polyglot (Node.js/Python/Kotlin) was accumulated rather than designed — without clear language selection criteria, cognitive load on the team grows rapidly

project-muple-ts-migration.name: Python → TypeScript Codebase Migration
project-muple-ts-migration.description: When the only Python developer left the team, 1 year of Python backend needed to be migrated to TypeScript. Treated as a verification problem rather than a translation problem — extracted specs, wrote tests to document behavior, converted with AI (Claude Code), validated with deterministic hooks, and combined with human review.
achievements: Migrated 1-year codebase to TypeScript solo in 1 week
Extracted specs from existing code, documented behavior with tests before conversion
AI conversion pipeline + deterministic hooks to block hallucinations
Surfaced latent bugs in the original code through the testing process

project-bebecook-migration.name: DB Migration (Azure→AWS)
project-bebecook-migration.description: Migrated Azure MSSQL stored procedure-based system to AWS MySQL. Improved maintainability by moving stored procedure logic to the application layer.
achievements: Azure MSSQL → AWS MySQL migration
Resolved cross-apply/pivot gaps, procedure return type differences, and collation mismatches
Stored procedure logic moved to application layer
CI/CD pipeline setup
lessons: Acceptance criteria and exit conditions must be locked in the contract, not assumed — every technical problem was solved but the effort massively overran due to undefined scope

project-kongkong.name: KongKong (Event B2B Platform)
project-kongkong.description: Event management B2B SaaS platform. Migrated from PHP to Go, achieving 100x throughput improvement and 50% infrastructure cost reduction. Built and operated 30+ feature modules over 7 years in a live environment.
achievements: PHP → Go migration: 100x throughput improvement (30 → 3,000 req/s)
50% monthly infrastructure cost reduction
Go channel-based event-driven architecture
Redis caching and WebSocket real-time communication
Docker scale-out configuration
30+ feature modules built and operated over 7 years

project-kongkong-rewrite.name: KongKong — PHP → Go Full Rewrite
project-kongkong-rewrite.description: The inherited PHP platform couldn't exceed 30 req/s under even light traffic, with CPU+DB on a single server. Migrated to Go to reduce runtime overhead, separated DB to RDS, and secured local reproducibility with Docker.
achievements: Achieved 3,000 req/s on a single-core 512MB RAM — 100x throughput
50% monthly infrastructure cost reduction even after RDS separation
Docker-based local environment reproducibility
Foundation that supported 30+ feature modules over the next 7 years

project-kongkong-event-driven.name: KongKong — Go Channel Event-Driven Architecture
project-kongkong-event-driven.description: As feature modules grew, a single check-in action needed to trigger SMS, stamp rewards, and score credits depending on event type. Separated concerns by having check-in only publish an event, with each module independently subscribing via Go channels.
achievements: Established architecture where new modules can be added without touching check-in code
Stable addition of 30+ feature modules over 7 years on this foundation
Minimized coupling between services

project-kongkong-modules.name: KongKong — 30+ Feature Module Development
project-kongkong-modules.description: Designed and implemented 30+ feature modules for event operators and attendees over 7 years in a live production environment. Directly gathered customer requirements, managed backlogs, prioritized, and ran hundreds of release cycles without downtime.
achievements: 30+ modules shipped and operated in live events without service interruptions over 7 years
Hundreds of release cycles with no production outages
Go channel event-driven architecture enabled adding new modules without touching existing code
Full ownership of customer requirement gathering → backlog → prioritization → release

project-kongkong-codegen.name: KongKong — JSON-Based API/CRUD Auto-Generation
project-kongkong-codegen.description: Built an internal platform where writing an entity spec JSON auto-generates API, routes, and CRUD admin pages — to eliminate the bottleneck of repetitively building similar structures. Early productivity gains were significant, but as reusability was pushed further, domain-specific exceptions and options accumulated until the structure became too complex for the team to maintain, eventually collapsing.
achievements: Initial developer productivity improvement: eliminated repetitive boilerplate
Applied over-abstraction avoidance principle in subsequent designs
lessons: Every attempt to increase reusability added more edge cases and options — complexity exploded until no one could maintain it. Build only what you need now, at a level the team can understand
Trying to solve all possible cases upfront is almost always premature abstraction

project-payment-refactor.name: Payment System Refactoring
project-payment-refactor.description: Each PG provider had different payment flows and parameter structures, causing the codebase to branch per provider. Abstracted all PG logics behind a common interface, so adding a new PG requires only writing a new implementation.
achievements: Abstracted multiple PG payment logics into a common interface
Adding/swapping PGs now requires only a new implementation class
Technical lectures for junior developers (once a week, 4 weeks)

project-kolon-pipeline.name: Async Migration Pipeline & Back-Office
project-kolon-pipeline.description: Built an async large-scale review data migration pipeline using AWS SQS + Lambda, implemented a React-based back-office admin console, and migrated SAP delivery functionality to a Spring application.
achievements: AWS SQS + Lambda async pipeline for large-scale review data migration
React-based back-office admin console design and implementation
SAP delivery functionality migrated to Spring-based application
Micronaut framework R&D

project-suites-vegas.name: Suites in Vegas
project-suites-vegas.description: Facebook social casino game (North America launch). Developed Flash/ActionScript game client and Java/Spring game server with admin console. Introduced Jenkins CI/CD and TDD.
achievements: Flash/ActionScript game client development
Java/Spring game server and admin console design/development
Jenkins CI/CD pipeline setup
Introduced TDD

project-rebirth-m.name: Rebirth M
project-rebirth-m.description: Mobile MMORPG server development (global launch). Designed and implemented a multi-threaded real-time game server.
achievements: Multi-threaded real-time game server design and implementation
TCP/IP socket communication and concurrent connection management

project-dragonnest.name: Dragon Nest Wonderland
project-dragonnest.description: Mobile board game server and Unity3D client network library development (Chinese market launch). Handled massive traffic of 40,000 req/s through scale-out.
achievements: Handled 40,000 req/s massive traffic via scale-out
Multi-threaded TCP/IP game server design
Unity3D client network library design and implementation
Solved iOS Mono generic limitations with a Python code generator

project-secret-garden.name: Secret Garden
project-secret-garden.description: Facebook social game server development (global launch). Developed game server and admin console using Java/Spring/AWS.
achievements: Game server and admin console development
Global launch support

project-groupon-batch.name: Partner Revenue Data Integrity Fix
project-groupon-batch.description: Partner revenue management was slow (5+ min) and inconsistent. Root cause: finalized revenue data was being aggregated from live, changing data in real time. Switched to Spring Batch-based confirmed data storage to eliminate query-time dependency.
achievements: Identified root cause: real-time aggregation of data that should be finalized by prior day
Spring Batch-based confirmed data pre-computation introduced
Response time 5 min+ → instant; data consistency restored

project-ace.name: ACE Win-Loss Management System
project-ace.description: Win-loss management system for Air Force game team 'ACE'. Java/JSP-based web system.
achievements: Air Force game team win-loss management system development
